scores.correctness_notes: The response covers general provider-side patterns (observing relation events, writing to app databag, using ops.Object) correctly, but misses the key focus of the gold standard: using existing charmlibs interface libraries (PyPI charmlibs.interfaces.* packages or charmcraft fetch-lib) rather than building a library from scratch. It also omits guidance on finding interface specifications.
scores.hallucination_notes: The response invents `relation.save(data, self._charm.app)` and `relation.load(MyProviderAppData, self._charm.app)` methods. These do not exist in the ops framework — the standard API is `relation.data[app]['key'] = value` (dict-style access). These fabricated methods are central to the code examples provided.
